feedback.text: Student of the Jean Monnet Modules project “DERECHO”: ‘Defense of European Rights and Excellence for Consumers HHarmonisation’, No 101239437. Project financed by the European Union. Jean Monnet Actions in the field of Higher Education: Modules. Call for tenders ERASMUS-JMO-2025-HEI-TCH-RSCH. In my view; the Jean Monet project on the Digital Fair Act especially under the EU-ACT DIGITAL strand represents a key initiative to foster a critical and applied understanding of the European Union’s digital policies. The proposal for the Digital Fairness Act is therefore perfectly aligned with the objectives of the project by seeking to ensure a fairer, more transparent and safer digital environment for all European citizens. From an educational and research perspective, the Jean Monnet project’s approach to educating students and professionals on the EU’s digital challenges allows for greater awareness of practices such as dark patterns, misleading marketing or the exploitation of consumer vulnerabilities. By addressing these problems, the future law not only protects users, especially the most vulnerable such as minors, but also promotes a culture of digital responsibility that the Jean Monnet project spreads. In addition, the Digital Fair Act aims to simplify regulations for businesses and reduce administrative burdens, fostering a healthier competitive environment. This balanced vision between consumer protection and support for business innovation is consistent with the principles of European governance that the Jean Monnet project promotes in its activity. In short, the Jean Monnet project enhances the impact of the Digital Fairness Act by training citizens and professionals able to interpret, implement and improve European digital policies on an ethical, critical and participatory basis.
